observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the municipal government should expedite its negotiation with the GSIS in order to come up with payment terms for the settlement of the obligation pursuant to Sections 3.1, 4.1.2, 5(c) and 14.1, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation of the Republic Act No.8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997, and provide the audit team a copy of the break-down of the Due to GSIS balance.

We recommended the management to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution of contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Therefore, we recommended that the LCE direct:

a. The Inventory Committee to conduct yearly physical count of all PPEs
owned by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of MNGAS, for
LGUs, Volume I.

b. The Accounting Division and the General Services Division or the
Designated Property and Supply Officer to maintain the PPELC and
PC, respectively, and a periodic reconciliation be made to detect any
discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records and
reflected in the book of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

a. provide the audit team a copy of the break-down of the Due to GSIS
balance to support the obligation in adherence to Section 4(6) of PD
1445.

b. expedite its negotiation with the GSIS in order to come up with
payment terms for the settlement of the obligation pursuant to
Sections 3.1, 4.1.2, 5(c) and 14.1, Rule III of the IRR of RA 8291.

In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended and Management
agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its
structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution of
contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and
3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere faithfully to the
provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009
on RRSA in the submission of the aforecited reports. The Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, should exert extra effort to
cope up with the deadline set for submission of said reports, so that
delay will be minimized, if not totally avoided in the future.

In view of the above, we recommended and Management agreed to the
following:

a) Require the Local Finance Committee to re-evaluate the target settings
by considering the past years’ actual collections in the preparation of
the budget for the succeeding year to come up with a viable and realistic
budget.

b) Require the departments serving as the collecting arms of the
Municipality to improve collection efficiency particularly the
Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) where the actual revenue and
receipts realized during the year was low.

c) Undertake massive tax collection campaign especially accounts with
material low realization rate involving key personnel of the Municipality,
such as but not limited to the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer
including some of revenue collectors, Municipal Engineer and others.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s index card as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable, and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments upon collection, pursuant to Sections 20, 84 and 86 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
We recommended the management to implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended the management to procure common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM in compliance to Section 53.5 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order (AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 201. If the same were not available, secure certification of non-availability of the items being procured.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable, and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments upon collection, pursuant to Sections 20, 84 and 86 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the accounting division.

update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property

Accountant in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable, and the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjustments upon collection, pursuant to
Sections 20, 84 and 86 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

2.8 We recommended and the management agreed to implement the
perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with
Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

3.6 We reiterated our recommendation that the municipal government
should expedite its negotiation with the GSIS in order to come up with
payment terms for the settlement of the obligation pursuant to Sections
3.1, 4.1.2, 5(c) and 14.1, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and
Regulation of the Republic Act No.8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act
of 1997, and provide the audit team a copy of the break-down of the Due to

4.2 We recommended that the management should direct all responsible
persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into

5.5 We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use
supplies directly from the PS-DBM in compliance to Section 53.5 of the
2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the
Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order
(AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 201. If the same were not available, secure
certification of non-availability of the items being procured.

6.5 In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended and the management
agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its
structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution of
contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and
3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

1.7 Recommendations:

a. Management should conduct a regular physical inventory of its
Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the RPCPPE
18
thereafter, pursuant to the provision of Section 156 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b. A periodic reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards maintained by the
accountant with that of the Property Cards maintained by the
General Services Officer should be regularly made to promptly
detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both
records should be out rightly recorded and reflected in the book of
accounts.

2.5 We recommended and the management agreed that the municipal
records be reconciled with that of GSIS to determine the exact amount of Due to GSIS account immediately remit the said amount, pursuant to Sections 4.1.2, 14.1 and 5 (c) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997.

3.3 We recommended that the municipal government should reconcile the
records with that of Pag-IBIG to determine the correct amount of the
obligation and settle immediately in accordance with Rule IV, Section
a 3 of Republic Act No. 9679, otherwise known as the Home Development
Mutual Fund Law of 2009 or Pag-IBIG Fund. We also commended the
Management for the effort of paying the remittances to Pag-IBIG.

4.5 We recommend that the management should observe the limitations
prescribed under Section 325(h) of the Republic Act No. 7160,
otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

5.9 We recommended that the management should implement the
perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with
Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

6.7 We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of
Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 on the Rules
and Regulations on Settlements of Accounts in the submission of the
aforecited reports. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer, should exert extra effort to cope up with the deadline set for
submission of said reports, so that delay will be minimized, if not totally
avoided in the future.

7.2 We recommended that the management should direct all responsible
persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into
disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

11.7 We commended the Municipality of Northern Kabuntalan
Management for the effort of paying all of their GSIS remittances
amounting to ₱ 7,408,091.73 during the year, in compliance with
Sections 3.1, 4.1.2 and 14.1 of the Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise
known as Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997 thus,
enabling its officials and employees in availing of the benefits and
privileges offered by the GSIS to state workers.

12.5 We commended the Municipality of Northern Kabuntalan
Management for the effort of paying all of their HDMF remittances
amounting to ₱ 959,374.70 during the year, in compliance with Rule
IV, Section 1 and Section 3 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 9679,
otherwise known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009
or Pag-IBIG Fund.

13.6 We commended the Management for the effort of paying all of their
Philhealth remittances amounting to ₱ 722,903.22 during the year, in
compliance with RA 10606, thus enabling its officials and employees in
availing of the benefits and privileges offered by the Philhealh to state
workers.

We recommended the Management to: (a) request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts as provided for in Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to carefully and diligently follow the provisions under the said COA Circular which gives the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances and Fund Transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement in accordance to paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

with Section 21 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, otherwise known as An Act

We recommend that the Management must ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommend that the following: a) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the duly certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant as a basis for the computation of RPT and SET Receivables. b) instruct the Accountant to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
We recommend to the management to submit all the documents on LDRRMF for our examination and evaluation, pursuant to Republic Act No. 2011.
We recommend that the management will settle the Notice Suspension (NS) before the 90 days period pursuant to COA-Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommend that the Management must ensure the proper granting, utilization
and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the

conduct detailed analysis of the CIP account and make the necessary entries, if any to
adjust the balances thereof. Registries and Subsidiary Ledgers should be maintained.

Recommendation:

2.8. The municipal government should communicate with the GSIS and reconcile
their records in order to come up with the true balance of the account and remit the same
thereafter.

2.9 The management is continuously negotiating for the settlement of the obligation.
Accordingly, a compromise agreement has been reached but the municipal government
does not have the capacity to pay for the monthly amortization demanded by the GSIS.
Nevertheless, management assured the audit team that it is doing its best to settle the
obligation.

Recommendation:

3.4 The Municipal Accountant should reconcile the beginning balance of the Loans
Payable – Domestic account with the bank and make the necessary adjustments.

3.5 Accordingly, the difference in the beginning balance maybe because of other
charges imposed by the bank upon the approval of the compromise arrangement like
payment of documentary stamp tax. To be sure, the management will communicate with
the bank and reconcile their differences.

5.5 We recommend that the Management should strictly observe the deadline of
remittance set forth in Rule VII, Section 3 of IRR of RA No. 9679 in order to avoid
unnecessary incurrence of penalties.

Recommendation:

4.4. We recommend that the management will settle the Notice Suspension (NS) within
the 90 days period pursuant to COA-Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

7.5. The management assured us that those unsettled Notice of Suspensions issued
will be prioritized by the responsible persons to avoid maturing in to disallowance.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following:
1. Adhere to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
2, Create an Inventory Team to conduct the physical inventory taking.

We recommend to the management to submit pertinent information and records pertaining to the loan contracted with the Philippine National Bank like copy of loan and credit financing agreement with the lender, general ledger of the Loans Payable-Domestic account and the information provided in the Statement of Debt Service of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: A Compromise agreement was reached and payment of amortization has been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management will settle the Notice Suspension (NS) within the 90 days period pursuant to COA-Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record all related transactions completely, properly and classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports, paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents pursuant to paragraph 7.3 of COA Circular No.94-006 dated February 12, 1994, as amended and Section 122 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend that Management instruct:

a)    The Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements 	(BRS) as at   Year End and on a monthly basis, as provided under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.	

b)  For the Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the GSIS contribution totaling to ₱11,414,600.95, pursuant to Section 3.4, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of the Republic Act No.8291, otherwise known as GSIS Act of 1997.

observations.recommendation:
Pursuant to Section 04(n) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (For Local Government Units), we recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting journal entry to correct the Statement of Financial Position for CY 2014, as follows:  

Gov’t Equity 					4,463,216.26
Pub. Infa	4,463,216.26
 To transfer Public Infrastructure to the Registry of Public 
Infrastructure at year end.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Accountant to disclose fully in the Notes to Financial Statement the amount and details of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as of every year-end reporting period and to adhere strictly with the provisions of related laws and regulations thereof.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following:
Adhere to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
Create an Inventory Team to conduct the physical inventory taking.

The LGU and GSIS should expedite the reconciliation in order to determine the right amount to be paid to the GSIS and any amount due should be remitted pursuant to the provision of Section 3.4, Rule III of IRR of GSIS Act of 1997 without prejudice to any charges that may be filed by responsible concerned officials of the LGU pursuant to the provision of RA 8291.

We recommend to the management to submit pertinent information and records pertaining to the loan contracted with the Philippine National Bank like copy of loan and credit financing agreement with the lender, general ledger of the Loans Payable-Domestic account and the information provided in the Statement of Debt Service of the Municipality.
We recommend that the management will settle the Notice Suspension (NS) within the 90 days period pursuant to COA-Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recognize the LGU of Kabuntalan, Maguindanao for their effort in the implementation of the Gender and Development (GAD) for CY 2016 in accordance with Section 35 of the Republic Act No.10717, otherwise known as the FY 2016 General Appropriations and likewise, recommend the said LGU to sustain the implementation of the GAD program in the succeeding years.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

We recommend that management should consider and include all aspects concerning its development not only in terms of economic but social and environmental as well so that the constituents of the Municipality of Kabuntalan will receive optimal benefits.  LGU-Kabuntalan should strictly follow the guidelines set forth in the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports, paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents pursuant to paragraph 7.3 of COA Circular No.94-006 dated February 12, 1994, as amended and Section 122 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

For the General Fund, we recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to constantly monitor and direct the personnel concerned to submit the paid disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents earlier so that the audit team can conduct the post audit and subsequently, issue Notice of Suspension if there is/are any deficiencies noted to avoid similar recurrence this coming year.   As to the Trust Fund, we recommend that the management will settle the Notice Suspension (NS) within the 90 days period pursuant to COA-Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Notices of suspensions issued were settled.
observations.reason: There is still delay in the submission of financial reports and disbursement vouchers
